perturbation.original_text: I/we further agree that in consideration of that certain information of this agreement that I/we fully agree to hold all information confidential and that such information will remain the express property of THE COMPANIES.
perturbation.changed_text: I/we further agree that in consideration of that certain information of this agreement that I/we agree to hold all information confidential for a period of 2 years and that such information will remain the express property of THE COMPANIES.
perturbation.explanation: By adding a time limit of “2 years” to the confidentiality agreement, an in-text contradiction is created. The original agreement states that the confidential information remains the express property of “THE COMPANIES”, implying perpetuity. Limiting the confidentiality period to two years introduces a contradiction, because after that period it is no longer bound to this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I/we further agree that in consideration of that certain information of this agreement that I/we agree to hold all information confidential for a period of 2 years and that such information will remain the express property of THE COMPANIES.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a time limit of 2 years to the confidentiality agreement, while the original text states the information remains the express property of 'THE COMPANIES', implying perpetuity. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating that this modification introduces a conflicting term. The 2-year limit directly contradicts the concept of the information remaining the companies' express property indefinitely, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the obligation is now time-bound and conflicts with the original notion of perpetual ownership. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: It is understood that this agreement is a reciprocal one between the signatories concerning the exchange of privileged information and contacts and will be held as confidential unless otherwise released or agreed as to the release only in written documentation by THE COMPANIES.
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood that this agreement is a reciprocal one between the signatories concerning the exchange of privileged information and contacts. Signatories are free to disclose privileged information and contacts to third parties at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states information exchanged "will be held as confidential unless otherwise released or agreed as to the release only in written documentation by THE COMPANIES." By removing this constraint, it introduces a direct contradiction. The original paragraph states it must be released by THE COMPANIES to do so, not at the Signatories' discretion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood that this agreement is a reciprocal one between the signatories concerning the exchange of privileged information and contacts. Signatories are free to disclose privileged information and contacts to third parties at their discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text establishes that the exchange of privileged information is confidential and can only be released with written consent from THE COMPANIES. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that signatories are free to disclose this information at their discretion, removing the confidentiality requirement and the need for consent from THE COMPANIES. This creates a direct conflict, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. Since the changed text and the contradicted text are in the same location, it exacerbates the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: It is also understood that a signatory cannot be considered or adjudged to be in violation of this agreement when the violation is involuntary, due to the situations beyond his/her control: examples being acts of GOD and/or civil disturbances. Essentially, the spirit behind this agreement is one of mutual trust and confidence, and one of reliance on each other to do what is fair and equitable.
perturbation.changed_text: It is also understood that a signatory is responsible for all violations of this agreement, regardless of whether the violation is involuntary or due to situations beyond his/her control, even acts of GOD and/or civil disturbances. Essentially, the spirit behind this agreement is one of strict liability.
perturbation.explanation: The original text offers an exception for involuntary violations outside a signatory's control (e.g., acts of God). The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by asserting that signatories are liable for ALL violations. This shift from an excusable involuntary violation to strict liability creates uncertainty about the agreement's enforceability and the nature of liability under unforeseen circumstances. This negates the mutual trust and equitable spirit of the original.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is also understood that a signatory is responsible for all violations of this agreement, regardless of whether the violation is involuntary or due to situations beyond his/her control, even acts of GOD and/or civil disturbances. Essentially, the spirit behind this agreement is one of strict liability.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text states that a signatory is responsible for all violations, regardless of whether they are involuntary or due to circumstances beyond their control. This directly contradicts the Original Text, which provided an exception for involuntary violations outside a signatory's control (e.g., acts of God). The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are essentially the same, emphasizing the complete shift in liability. The 'Perturbation Explanation' highlights this shift from excusable involuntary violation to strict liability and negates the mutual trust of the original agreement. This contradiction fits the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition because different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations and penalties regarding liability for violations.

perturbation.original_text: If either party sues the other party to enforce any of the terms of this agreement, the prevailing party shall, in addition to all other damages, be entitled to recover any and all legal fees incurred.
perturbation.changed_text: If either party sues the other party to enforce any of the terms of this agreement, each party shall bear its own legal fees, regardless of the outcome.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause states the "prevailing party" shall recover legal fees. The altered clause dictates "each party shall bear its own legal fees, regardless of the outcome." This directly conflicts with the prior paragraph's intent to fully indemnify the winning party in legal actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either party sues the other party to enforce any of the terms of this agreement, each party shall bear its own legal fees, regardless of the outcome.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a direct contradiction. The original clause stated that the prevailing party in a lawsuit would recover legal fees. The changed text dictates that each party bears its own legal fees, regardless of the outcome. This directly conflicts with the original intent of indemnifying the winning party in legal actions, making the changed text and the contradicted text incompatible. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.
